I told myself I would sleep early tonight.
That was several hours ago.
I keep checking the time like it owes me something.
The problem with being awake this late is having too much time to think.
I think my sleep schedule gave up on me.
At this point I am not even fighting it anymore.
Everything feels quieter after midnight.
Not silent. Just quieter.
I always notice the clock more at night.
Maybe because it feels louder.
I was tired all day.
Now that I can sleep, I am suddenly awake.
I wonder how many people are staring at a ceiling right now.
The night always feels longer when you are counting hours.
I should stop looking at the time.
I say that every night.
Sometimes I stay awake for no reason at all.
Sometimes there is a reason.
Neither helps.
The city looks different when most people are asleep.
Like it belongs to someone else.
I remember when staying up late felt exciting.
Now it just feels familiar.
The strange thing is that I am tired.
Just not sleepy.
There is a difference.
I have had entire conversations with myself at three in the morning.
Some were surprisingly productive.
Most were not.
The best ideas arrive when I should be sleeping.
The worst ones do too.
I always think tomorrow will fix everything.
Tomorrow usually has other plans.
There is something comforting about seeing lights still on in other windows.
It reminds me I am not the only one awake.
The sky before sunrise is my favorite color.
I just wish I saw it less often.
I keep making deals with myself.
Just one more hour.
The hour never stays one hour.
Coffee is not helping anymore.
I think it knows I am dependent on it.
Every night feels shorter than it should.
Every morning arrives too fast.
I miss being excited about waking up early.
Maybe I will get there again.
Some nights are peaceful.
Others refuse to let my thoughts sit still.
I wonder what it feels like to fall asleep instantly.
That sounds like a superpower.
The funny thing is that I love the night.
The night just does not love me back.
I have reached the point where birds sound like an alarm clock.
That is never a good sign.
I always think more clearly when everyone else is asleep.
Maybe that is part of the problem.
The night has a way of making everything feel important.
Even things that are not.
I should probably sleep.
Maybe in a little while.
